Oracc: SAAo/SAA01 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West

SAA 01 204. Tracking a Ninevite Scribe (ABL 0706+)[via saao/saa01]

Obverse
o 1o 1

a-na LUGAL be-lí-ía

(1) To the king, my lord: your servant Zeru-ibni. Good health to the king, my lord!

o 22

ARAD-ka mNUMUN—DÙ

o 33

lu DI-mu a-na LUGAL EN-ía

o 44

ina UGU-ḫi mdÌR.RA*—ga-mil

(4) As to Erra-gamil, the Ninevite scribe about whom the king, my lord, wrote to me: "He is there; summon him, get hold of him and send him to [the revi]ew!" —

o 55

LÚv.A.BA DUMU—URU.ni-nu-a

o 66

ša ⸢LUGAL⸣ be-lí iš-pur-an-ni

o 77

ma-a ḫa*-⸢na*-ka⸣ šu-u-tú

o 88

ma-a re-e-⸢su* i⸣-ši

o 99

ma-a ṣa-bat ⸢ina⸣ [ma-šar]-te*

o 1010

še-bi-la-šú [re]-⸢e⸣-su

(10) I summoned him, and thoroughly questioned Nabû-šumu-uṣur and the scribes from Nemed-Ištar and Laqê, servants of the king my lord, (who told me):

o 1111

a-ti-ši a-na mdPA—MU—PAB

o 1212

a-na LÚv.A.BA-MEŠ

o 1313

URU.né-med—d15-a.a

o 1414

a-na LÚv.A.BA-MEŠ

o 1515

KUR.la-qa-a.a

o 1616

LÚv.ARAD-MEŠ ša LUGAL

Bottom
b.e. 17b.e. 17

be-lí-ía a-sa-al

b.e. 1818

ú-ta-ṣi-ṣi

Reverse
r 1r 1

ma-a ina ša-lu-še-ni

(r 1) "He came two years ago, got a position with Ilai-Bel, and worked for a year on his behalf. Last year, while Ilai-Bel still was alive, a tracker came and took him away. They deported him to the Land beyond the River."

r 22

i-tal-ka ma-a* ina pa-an

r 33

mDINGIR-a.a—EN it-ti-ti-zi

r 44

ma-a ṭup-pi-šu dul-lu

r 55

ina UGU-ḫi-šú e-tap-áš

r 66

ma-a ina MU.IM.MA

r 77

ma-a ina TI.⸢LA*⸣-ma ša* mDINGIR-a.a—EN

r 88

ma-a LÚv.UŠ—kib-si i-tal-ka

r 99

i-ti-ši-šu

r 1010

ma-a a-na KUR.e-bir—ÍD

r 1111

ú-sa-ga-li-uš

rest uninscribed

Adapted from Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West (State Archives of Assyria, 1), 1987. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P334499/.
